events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Sir Richard Starkey, known as Ringo Starr, the iconic drummer for The Beatles, celebrates his 86th birthday.

angles.name: Ringo's Beat
angles.copy: Eighty-six years young! Ringo Starr, the legendary Beatle, is still keeping the beat going strong. What's your favourite Ringo moment?

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist and astrophysicist Sir Brian May celebrates his 79th birthday.

angles.name: Rock God Turns 79
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the one and only Brian May! From stadium-rocking riffs to actual astrophysics, the Queen legend truly does it all.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The charismatic frontman of The Rolling Stones, Sir Mick Jagger, celebrates his 83rd birthday.

angles.name: Jagger's Still Got It
angles.copy: Eighty-three years young and still dancing! Wishing a very happy birthday to the incomparable Mick Jagger. What's your ultimate Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) first launched in the United States on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its inaugural music video.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago today, MTV changed music forever. Remember tuning in just for the videos? The Buggles certainly got it right with that first one.

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which quickly became a Britpop classic.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Anniversary
angles.copy: It's been 32 years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and changed the sound of British music. What's your favourite track from this iconic debut?

events.title: Woodstock Festival took place (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in music history, took place from August 15–18, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Remembering Woodstock
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people gathered for three days of peace, love, and music at Woodstock. What an era!

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death (1977)
events.blurb: Today marks 49 years since the passing of the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, on August 16, 1977.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Forty-nine years on, the legacy of Elvis Presley continues to inspire. The King may have left the building, but his music lives on.

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's death (1996)
events.blurb: Today marks 30 years since the tragic death of influential rapper Tupac Shakur, who was fatally shot in Las Vegas on September 7, 1996, and died six days later.

angles.name: Remembering Tupac
angles.copy: Thirty years ago, the music world lost a giant. Remembering Tupac Shakur and his profound impact on hip-hop and beyond.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death (1970)
events.blurb: Today marks 56 years since the passing of guitar virtuoso Jimi Hendrix on September 18, 1970.

angles.name: Remembering Jimi Hendrix
angles.copy: Fifty-six years since we lost the incomparable Jimi Hendrix. His groundbreaking guitar work still sounds as fresh and electrifying today.

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles released their eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', on September 26, 1969, featuring iconic tracks like 'Come Together' and 'Something'.

angles.name: Abbey Road Anniversary
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That zebra crossing is still iconic, and so is every track on it. What's your favourite?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, the iconic frontwoman of The Pretenders, celebrates her 75th birthday.

angles.name: Hynde's Still Rocking
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock icon, still delivering those powerful Pretenders hits.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Liam Gallagher, the distinctive voice of Oasis and a formidable solo artist, celebrates his 54th birthday.

angles.name: Our Kid Turns 54
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing that rock 'n' roll swagger. What's your favourite Liam track, solo or Oasis?

events.title: John Lennon released Imagine (1971)
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic album 'Imagine' was released on September 9, 1971, featuring the timeless title track.

angles.name: Imagine Turns 55
angles.copy: Fifty-five years ago, John Lennon gave us 'Imagine' – an album that still resonates with its message of peace and hope.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: On October 23, 2001, Apple introduced the first-generation iPod, revolutionising portable music players.

angles.name: iPod's 25th Anniversary
angles.copy: Remember the click wheel? Twenty-five years ago, Apple launched the original iPod, putting '1,000 songs in your pocket' and changing how we listen to music forever.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated annually as James Bond Day, marking the release date of the first 007 film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Happy Bond Day
angles.copy: Grab your martini, shaken not stirred! It's James Bond Day, celebrating 64 years since 007 first hit the big screen. What's your favourite Bond theme tune?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Remembering John Lennon, who would have celebrated his 86th birthday today.

angles.name: Remembering John Lennon
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the visionary John Lennon. His music and message of peace continue to inspire the world.
